description_long: This house features a white stucco exterior with a textured stone-like finish, accented by tan trim around the windows and corners. The roof is covered with reddish-orange terracotta tiles, sloping in multiple directions and featuring a prominent chimney. The structure is two stories tall, with a symmetrical arrangement of square and rectangular windows on each facade, some grouped in rows and others spaced apart. The main entryway is a simple rectangular opening without a porch or steps, located on the ground floor. The rear and side facades display a mix of window sizes and decorative stonework, giving the building a Mediterranean or Southern European appearance. The overall form is rectangular with a slight L-shaped extension, and the roofline is complex but dominated by a gable style.
description: A two-story white stucco house with a red terracotta tile gable roof, tan trim, and symmetrical window arrangements. The design is Mediterranean-inspired with a simple main entryway and decorative stonework.
description_view_0: Front view showing three windows on the upper floor, two on the lower, and a main entryway on the right side.
description_view_1: Side and partial rear view revealing the L-shaped extension, chimney, and a row of small square windows along the back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the gable end, two upper windows, one lower window, and the entryway.
description_view_3: Rear view with a long facade, five upper windows, four lower windows, and a row of small decorative windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with some grouped small windows
attribute_entryway_design: Simple rectangular main door with no porch or steps, framed by tan trim
